name: Cettiidae
description: Cettiidae is a family of small, plain-colored bush warblers found primarily in Asia. They are secretive birds that inhabit dense undergrowth and are more often heard than seen.

children.name: Horornis
children.description: A genus of medium-sized bush warblers with strong legs and unstreaked plumage, found across Asia and Oceania.

children.name: Horornis diphone
children.description: The Japanese Bush Warbler is an olive-brown warbler with pale underparts and a famously loud, whistled song. It is beloved in Japan for its melodic calls heralding spring.

children.name: Horornis fortipes
children.description: The Brown-flanked Bush Warbler has warm brown flanks and a long, pale supercilium. It inhabits dense thickets in montane forests from the Himalayas to Taiwan.

children.name: Horornis canturians
children.description: The Manchurian Bush Warbler is a plain brown warbler with a strong, melodic song. It breeds in northeast Asia and winters in Southeast Asia.

children.name: Horornis flavolivaceus
children.description: The Aberrant Bush Warbler is an olive-green warbler with yellowish underparts and a distinctive face pattern. It skulks in dense undergrowth in montane forests of Southeast Asia.

children.name: Horornis parens
children.description: A bush warbler endemic to the Solomon Islands.

children.name: Horornis hadrophyllus
children.description: A bush warbler endemic to the Philippines.

children.name: Horornis anurus
children.description: A bush warbler found in the eastern Himalayas and Southeast Asia.

children.name: Horornis brunnescens
children.description: A bush warbler found in the eastern Himalayas.

children.name: Horornis acanthizoides
children.description: A bush warbler with yellowish belly, found in East Asia.

children.name: Horornis vulcanius
children.description: A bush warbler found in the montane forests of the Greater Sundas.

children.name: Horornis anurus
children.description: A bush warbler from the eastern Himalayas.

children.name: Horornis brunnescens
children.description: A bush warbler from the Himalayas.

children.name: Abroscopus
children.description: A genus of tiny, colorful warblers with distinctive face patterns, found in Asian forests.

children.name: Abroscopus albogularis
children.description: The Rufous-faced Warbler is a tiny, active warbler with a bright rufous face, white throat, and olive upperparts. It forages in mixed-species flocks in foothill forests from the Himalayas to Taiwan.

children.name: Abroscopus schisticeps
children.description: A tiny warbler with black face and bright yellow throat, found from the Himalayas to Vietnam.

children.name: Abroscopus superciliaris
children.description: A tiny warbler with yellow underparts, found from the Himalayas to Southeast Asia.

children.name: Tesia
children.description: A genus of tiny, brightly coloured warblers with long legs and short tails, found in the forests of Asia.

children.name: Tesia castaneocoronata
children.description: A tiny warbler with chestnut head and bright yellow underparts, found from the Himalayas to Vietnam.

children.name: Tesia olivea
children.description: A tiny warbler with slaty belly, found from the Himalayas to Vietnam.

children.name: Tesia superciliaris
children.description: A tiny warbler endemic to the montane forests of Java.

children.name: Tesia everetti
children.description: A tiny warbler with russet cap, found in the Lesser Sunda Islands.

children.name: Urosphena
children.description: A genus of small warblers with extremely short tails, found in East and Southeast Asia.

children.name: Urosphena squameiceps
children.description: A tiny warbler with very short tail and scaled crown, found in East Asia.

children.name: Urosphena subulata
children.description: A tiny warbler with short tail, found on Timor and nearby islands.

children.name: Urosphena whiteheadi
children.description: A tiny warbler endemic to the montane forests of Borneo.

children.name: Urosphena palpebrosa
children.description: A small bush warbler found in the Himalayas and Southeast Asia.

children.name: Urosphena canturians
children.description: A bush warbler breeding in northeast Asia and wintering in Southeast Asia.
